evidence_quotes.quote: disable or remove access to specific features of a software or whole software (including videogames).
Any always online DRM should be removed on the last patch.

paragraphs.text: In my opinion any digital purchase should be treated like physical purchase. Wether it is movie, tv-series, videogame, software or anything that has to do with along those lines. This should protect customer from any company being able to disable or remove access to specific features of a software or whole software (including videogames). When you purchase product and later company that sold the product decides to drop support I still should have access to product as it was when support ended. Any always online DRM should be removed on the last patch. There are multiple software, videogames, movies and tv-series that cannot be accessed legally anymore. All of them are and should be concidered as art. Any product sold on physical media should also have the product in playable/usable state as it was released. No need for day 1 patches.
